title: strychnos rondeletioides
intro: Strychnos rondeletioides, also known as the West African poison nut, is a plant species native to tropical West Africa, characterized by its toxic seeds. Traditionally, the plant has been used in African medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and intestinal worms. The seeds of the plant have been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, and are also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often dried and ground into a powder, which can be used to make teas or added to traditional remedies.
